subject: Relion5 Refine3D to High Resolution Refinement in M
body:
Hi all,

I have a question regarding transition from STA R5 to WarpM.
I did all processing in R5 and finished 3D refinement with Bin1 sub-volumes.
Now I want to push resolution (if possible) with WarpM.

I follow this tutorial and experienced problems with generating correct 
files to run MCore:
https://warpem.github.io/warp/user_guide/warptools/quick_start_warptools_tilt_series/#high-resolution-refinements-in-m

I can create population and source with MTools.
To create species I had to modify run_data.star from Refine3D, otherwise 
output folder species is empty.
Namely, I removed data_general and data_optics blocks in the run_data.star 
header and keep only data_particles block.
In addition, rlnOriginXYZAngst were renamed to rlnOriginXYZ, and 
rlnCenteredCoordinateXYZ to _rlnCoordinateXYZ.
Also, in "MTools create_species" I add --angpix_coords 1 and 
--angpix_shifts 1 options.
As a result, I see the folder with different types of maps (half12, mask, 
filt, filtsharp etc), .species file, fsc.star and noisenet.pt files.  But 
there is no generated _particles.star, and MCore crashes:

* MCore --population m/REF1_CLASS521_BIN1_v2.population  --iter 0Loading 
population... Unhandled exception. System.IO.FileNotFoundException: Could 
not find file 
'/data/Tomo/Titan3_DCI_Tomo_Set1_20240731/m/species/SET1_2cf852ed/SET1_particles.star'.*

I am using RELION 5.0-beta-commit-7d79f3
and MTools 2.0.0+9785ab84f87f75153c56992837a148aa69a2faad.
Refine3D original run_data.star contains these lines:
data_particles

loop_
_rlnTomoName #1
_rlnOpticsGroup #2
_rlnTomoParticleName #3
_rlnTomoVisibleFrames #4
_rlnImageName #5
_rlnOriginXAngst #6
_rlnOriginYAngst #7
_rlnOriginZAngst #8
_rlnCenteredCoordinateXAngst #9
_rlnCenteredCoordinateYAngst #10
_rlnCenteredCoordinateZAngst #11
_rlnGroupNumber #12
_rlnAngleRot #13
_rlnAngleTilt #14
_rlnAnglePsi #15
_rlnClassNumber #16
_rlnNormCorrection #17
_rlnRandomSubset #18
_rlnLogLikeliContribution #19
_rlnMaxValueProbDistribution #20
_rlnNrOfSignificantSamples #21

I will grateful for any tips, suggestions.

Best,
Sergey Nazarov,
DCI EPFL
Lausanne
